unsupportedAssumptions: Whether super_admin is intended to be global is not represented in the database.
Whether one user may belong to multiple clinics is not represented.
No clinic membership table or equivalent database object exists.
Clinic ownership for historical patients, traces, cycles, packs, audit rows and roles is not always derivable from captured schema alone.
Scanner/workstation authentication requirements are not fully specified by the schema.
No SteriSphere Storage bucket dependency is proven.
Exact authorization performed before app/setup/actions.ts constructs the service-role client requires dedicated review.

approvalGroups.title: Tenant identity and clinic membership model
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: user_roles maps one globally unique email to one role. It has no auth.users foreign key, clinic_id, or membership rows; multi-clinic scope is not representable.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Create an auth-user-linked active clinic-membership authority; treat super_admin as explicitly global and every other role as membership-scoped. Update TypeScript to consume server-authoritative membership.

impact.security: Replaces client/email trust with database identity.
impact.applicationRuntime: AuthGuard, AppShell, settings, assistant and investigation role reads must change.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Existing email role records need deterministic mapping; unsupported assumptions must not drive backfill.
impact.productionMigration: New membership objects and user_roles transition are required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Retain a reversible mapping and emergency audited owner bootstrap; never fall back to public role mutation.

preflightRequirements: Inventory auth users and user_roles by normalized email.
Identify orphan, duplicate, inactive and unknown-role records.
Owner must decide global super_admin and multi-clinic semantics.
validationRequirements: Cross-clinic denial and global super_admin audit.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve the membership model only with explicit bootstrap and migration mapping.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: JWT-only roles avoid a membership table but introduce claim-staleness and still need clinic scope.

approvalGroups.title: Anonymous and PUBLIC application access removal
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: cycles, packs, patients, patient_traces and user_roles have PUBLIC policies; the browser also uses an anon-key Supabase client for direct table access.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Remove anon/PUBLIC application-table access. Authenticated access must be membership/role scoped; service role remains server-only.

impact.security: Closes anonymous read/write and privilege-escalation paths.
impact.applicationRuntime: Direct browser queries will fail until authenticated policies and membership-aware code ship.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Login can remain public through Supabase Auth, but data pages/setup/scanner/workstation flows may break if they currently operate without a session.
impact.productionMigration: Policy migration and coordinated application rollout required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Feature-gated rollout; rollback may restore narrowly scoped authenticated access, never broad PUBLIC role mutation.

preflightRequirements: Trace every browser query and session expectation.
Confirm scanner/workstation authentication design.
Confirm setup/onboarding bootstrap path.
validationRequirements: Authenticated member success and non-member denial.
Login remains functional without table access.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve removal, conditional on application compatibility work before enforcement.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Keep narrowly scoped anonymous RPCs for proven kiosk workflows; do not retain direct table access.

approvalGroups.title: Role authorization and privilege administration
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: PUBLIC can insert, read and update user_roles. Client components interpret five role strings by email.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Replace user_roles policies with server-controlled membership mutation; permit self/authorized membership reads; only audited super_admin bootstrap and delegated clinic-admin operations may change roles.

impact.security: Eliminates anonymous privilege escalation and role enumeration.
impact.applicationRuntime: Settings user management and client role lookups need server APIs/membership responses.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Current direct .from("user_roles") queries will not survive unchanged.
impact.productionMigration: Application and Production policy/schema migration required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Maintain an audited break-glass owner process, not a PUBLIC policy.

preflightRequirements: Inventory every user_roles consumer and mutation.
Define role delegation matrix and first-super-admin owner.
validationRequirements: Delegation boundaries by role and clinic.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve replacement and require a server authorization API before policy enforcement.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: JWT claims can feed UI role display but should be issued from the same membership authority.

approvalGroups.title: Patient and traceability access
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: PUBLIC can insert/read patients and patient_traces. Neither table has clinic_id; trace relations do not establish an explicit tenant policy.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Add enforceable clinic ownership; allow clinic-member clinical roles to read and doctor/clinical_staff/admin roles to create; deny anon and cross-clinic access.

impact.security: Protects patient and trace data from anonymous disclosure/mutation.
impact.tenantIsolation: Requires new tenant keys or a proven immutable ownership chain.
impact.applicationRuntime: Patient pages/import, trace creation/history, reports and pack detail need authenticated membership context.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Current browser-direct queries will break until new policies and code are coordinated.
impact.productionMigration: Schema, data backfill and policy migration required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Preserve trace integrity and reversible tenant backfill; do not roll back to PUBLIC access.

preflightRequirements: Derive/verify clinic ownership for every patient and trace.
Identify ambiguous/orphan records and nullable links.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve only with a complete tenant backfill plan.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Derive trace tenant through a mandatory parent chain, but only if every path is non-null and indexed.

approvalGroups.title: Cycle, load, and pack access
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: cycles and packs allow broad PUBLIC operations; load_items allows all authenticated users. cycles lacks clinic_id and pack ownership can be ambiguous when cycle_id is nullable.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Require clinic ownership and membership; clinical_staff/admin perform mutations, approved clinical/auditor roles read, and trusted generation uses authenticated server/service workflows.

impact.security: Removes anonymous operational mutation and inventory disclosure.
impact.tenantIsolation: Adds/derives clinic ownership through mandatory indexed relationships.
impact.applicationRuntime: CycleWizard, cycle pages, pack generation/review, inventory, dashboard and investigations are affected.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Direct anon-key browser operations may fail; pack generation boundaries may need server actions.
impact.productionMigration: Schema/backfill, policy and application migration required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Feature-gate writes and preserve cycle/pack consistency; never restore unrestricted anonymous update.

preflightRequirements: Inventory null/orphan cycle and pack links.
Map each UI operation to authenticated browser or server execution.
validationRequirements: Concurrent pack generation and expired-review authorization.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve with coordinated server/browser workflow changes.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Use narrowly scoped workflow RPCs rather than direct client writes.

approvalGroups.title: Deployment, setup, provider, sterilizer, and clinic-settings authorization
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Authenticated users can read/write clinic settings, providers and sterilizers across clinics. Setup deployment server action uses service_role; policy names do not enforce admin.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Membership-scoped reads; admin/super_admin writes; deployment mutations remain service-role server operations with durable clinic checks.

impact.security: Prevents cross-clinic setup and configuration changes.
impact.tenantIsolation: Uses existing clinic_id but requires membership enforcement.
impact.applicationRuntime: Settings and setup browser reads plus server deployment flows are affected.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Service-role setup should continue; browser settings queries require membership-aware policies.
impact.productionMigration: Production policies must align before releasing membership-dependent setup UI.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Keep deployment recovery/idempotency evidence; roll back application feature flags, not tenant checks.

preflightRequirements: Confirm clinic_id completeness and valid settings/provider/sterilizer ownership.
Confirm setup server action authentication/authorization before it constructs service client.
validationRequirements: End-to-end deployment and recovery under service role.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve with an explicit server-action caller authorization gate.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Move all setup/configuration reads and writes behind server APIs.

approvalGroups.title: Runtime RPC and service-role execution
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: All 17 deployment RPCs are called by Supabase repositories composed through server modules and app/setup/actions.ts, which creates a client from SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. No browser-side .rpc call was found.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Preserve service-role-only execution; revoke PUBLIC/anon/authenticated; fixed trusted search_path; validate tenant identity from durable rows rather than p_clinic_id alone.

impact.security: Keeps ownership tokens and deployment mutations off the browser.
impact.tenantIsolation: Every RPC must compare clinic across run/session/item/entity/target rows.
impact.applicationRuntime: Current server flow is preserved if caller authorization and service credentials remain server-only.
impact.compatibilityRisk: No RPC call-site redesign expected; exact ACL and signature tests required.
impact.productionMigration: Baseline can encode current ACL; Production change only if attribute comparison finds drift.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Use existing idempotency/recovery semantics; never broaden execute roles as rollback.

preflightRequirements: Confirm exact function ACLs/security/search_path.
Trace server-action authorization before service-role construction.
validationRequirements: Cross-clinic, stale token/lease, replay, malformed input and concurrency tests.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve the service-role ACL matrix; require caller authorization audit.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Authenticated SECURITY DEFINER RPCs are possible but materially expand policy complexity.

approvalGroups.title: Trigger-helper permissions
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Eight live updated-at helpers execute as trigger functions; direct execute privileges/search paths require normalization.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Keep invoker trigger behavior, fixed trusted search_path, and deny direct PUBLIC/anon/authenticated execute.

impact.tenantIsolation: No tenant authorization is delegated to helpers.
impact.applicationRuntime: Transparent to application if triggers remain attached.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Trigger behavior preserved; ACL tightening may require Production migration.
impact.productionMigration: Later Production ACL/search_path migration expected where drift exists.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Restore only verified trigger attachment/behavior, not broad execute.

preflightRequirements: Compare helper definitions, ACLs and trigger attachments.
validationRequirements: Timestamp update and returned NEW row.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Keep default execute ACLs, with unnecessary attack surface.

approvalGroups.title: Patient external-ID uniqueness
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Two equivalent global unique constraints cover nullable patients.external_id; no clinic key participates.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Use one non-null clinic/source/external-id uniqueness rule; allow null draft identities.

impact.tenantIsolation: Scopes external identity to clinic and source.
impact.applicationRuntime: Patient import and manual creation conflict handling changes.
impact.productionMigration: Data preflight and later Production constraint migration required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Retain duplicate-resolution evidence and reversible constraint transition.

preflightRequirements: Find duplicate/null external IDs by clinic/source.
Resolve records without derivable clinic.
validationRequirements: Same-clinic collision, cross-clinic reuse, different-source reuse, null drafts, concurrency.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve clinic/source-scoped uniqueness after data inventory.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Keep global identity only if external IDs are proven globally issued.

approvalGroups.title: Provider and sterilizer uniqueness
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Normalized provider and sterilizer names are globally unique; non-null deployment keys are clinic-scoped.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Keep clinic-scoped deployment keys and make normalized names clinic-scoped for active/non-archived rows; permit planned null-key drafts.

impact.security: Avoids cross-clinic information coupling through uniqueness errors.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Existing same-clinic collisions and lifecycle transitions need preflight.
impact.productionMigration: Data preflight and later index migration required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Preserve resolved identity mappings; avoid recreating global uniqueness.

preflightRequirements: Inventory normalized names per clinic/lifecycle.
Check null deployment keys and archived rows.
validationRequirements: Same-clinic conflicts, lifecycle transitions and concurrent inserts.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Use stable provider/device IDs only and permit duplicate display names.

approvalGroups.title: Workstation ordering
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Production has non-unique (display_order,name); repository has display_order only.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Use Production composite index; allow ties/nulls and do not invent uniqueness.

impact.tenantIsolation: Ordering remains within query filtering; no tenant constraint change.
impact.productionMigration: No Production change for baseline authority.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Drop/recreate only after query-plan evidence if needed.

preflightRequirements: Confirm normalized Production index definition.
validationRequirements: Query plans and stable tie ordering.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Retain display_order-only index with less deterministic support.

approvalGroups.title: Hardware, printer, agent, and provisioning defaults
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Printer checks are NOT VALID; database defaults include localhost agent URL, Wi-Fi and port 9100; URLs and deployment identities are nullable across lifecycle states.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Valid clean-baseline constraints; environment owns endpoints/topology; deployment engine owns provisioning transitions; application owns product defaults; database enforces state-dependent valid persisted state.

impact.security: Reduces unsafe loopback/HTTP assumptions and invalid active states.
impact.tenantIsolation: Active hardware must have clinic ownership.
impact.applicationRuntime: Setup, print agent, local workstation and hardware deployment configuration change.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Local installs need explicit configuration; planned drafts remain nullable.
impact.productionMigration: Application/configuration change must precede Production default/constraint changes.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Retain last known explicit device config and deployment recovery plan.

preflightRequirements: Inventory invalid checks, localhost values, HTTP URLs and active rows missing identity.
Define local deployment mode.
validationRequirements: Local/remote, USB/network, planned/active/archived transitions and invalid combinations.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve source-of-truth split with explicit local-mode support.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Preserve DB defaults for single-machine deployments, accepting portability risk.

approvalGroups.title: Platform and Supabase prerequisites
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Supabase provides auth/storage/extensions schemas, platform roles and gen_random_uuid(); filtered capture emits no application-owned extension declaration.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Assert compatible platform schemas/roles/functions; reference auth.users/auth.uid(); never recreate platform internals or roles; exclude Storage until usage is proven.

impact.security: Keeps platform ownership and auth trust boundaries intact.
impact.tenantIsolation: Membership depends on auth identity availability.
impact.applicationRuntime: Login remains Supabase Auth; no current Storage application dependency is proven.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Fails fast on incompatible environment rather than partially initializing.
impact.productionMigration: No application Production schema change; baseline preflight requirement.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Abort initialization before mutation when assertions fail.

preflightRequirements: Verify versions, schemas, roles, auth helpers and UUID generation.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Application-own extensions only after platform documentation confirms necessity.

approvalGroups.title: Function, index, constraint, trigger, and default normalization
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Evolved and superseded definitions coexist; long identifiers truncate; function attributes need normalized comparison; selected constraints/defaults genuinely differ.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Use evolved Production-normalized clinics/runs/functions/triggers; ignore naming-only truncation; explicitly migrate genuine ACL, constraint and default differences; exclude core/planning history.

impact.security: Normalizes function ACL/search_path and avoids accidental superseded objects.
impact.applicationRuntime: Should preserve deployed behavior except separately approved uniqueness/default changes.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Raw text concatenation is prohibited; attribute equality must be proven.
impact.productionMigration: Later Production migration for genuine differences only.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Version normalized definitions and use forward correction, not raw baseline replay.

preflightRequirements: Catalog-normalize every function/index/constraint/trigger/default.
Separate truncation from semantic drift.
validationRequirements: Definition hashes, dependency graph, RPC signatures, triggers, constraints and counts.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Raw dump copying preserves noise and platform coupling and is not recommended.

approvalGroups.title: Audit access and tenant ownership
approvalGroups.currentBehavior: Any authenticated user may insert and read all audit rows; audit_logs has no clinic_id.
approvalGroups.recommendation: Add tenant ownership; service-role/trusted server insert only; clinic-scoped super_admin/admin/auditor read; no client update/delete.

impact.tenantIsolation: Requires clinic ownership plus an explicit global/system audit treatment.
impact.applicationRuntime: Audit writer, audit page, dashboard, reports and assistant activity change.
impact.compatibilityRisk: Current browser audit writes/reads may fail and historical rows need tenant classification.
impact.productionMigration: Security review, data backfill and later Production migration required.
impact.rollbackRecovery: Keep append-only evidence and quarantine unassigned historical rows; do not restore broad writes.

preflightRequirements: Inventory audit writers and historical tenant derivability.
Define global/system event ownership.
validationRequirements: Server insert and browser mutation denial.
approvalGroups.codexRecommendation: Approve only with a historical audit ownership plan.
approvalGroups.strongestAlternative: Keep global audit table accessible only to global super_admin/auditor.
